(5) iSAQB Modul Application Programming Interfaces (API)



API Design & Description in the Age of AI

Outline (API Design & Description in the Age of AI)

  1. Introduction [3]
  2. API Design & Description in the Age of AI [6]
  3. Exposing API Landscapes for AI Consumption [9]
  4. MCP to the Rescue!?! [4]
  5. Conclusions [2]
API Design & Description in the Age of AI Thilo Frotscher & Erik Wilde: APIs für die AI Nutzung: Wie geht das?

(7) Creating APIs (in the old days…) #1

  • Requirements Analysis
    • Talk to your consumers
    • Outside-in approach
  • API First
    • Imagine you are on a greenfield
    • Do not create APIs based on existing systems or use cases


API Design & Description in the Age of AI Thilo Frotscher & Erik Wilde: APIs für die AI Nutzung: Wie geht das?

(8) Creating APIs (in the old days…) #2

  • API Design
    • Use iterative approach of design – review - design
    • Implement good practices for API Design
    • Make sure to have API Design Guidelines in your organization
    • Focus on usability, consistency, comprehensibility, extensibility
    • Use naming conventions and consistent endpoint structures
    • Favor generic APIs over support for specific consumers / use cases


API Design & Description in the Age of AI Thilo Frotscher & Erik Wilde: APIs für die AI Nutzung: Wie geht das?

(9) Creating APIs (in the old days…) #3

  • API specification
    • Use standard description languages, like OpenAPI or AsyncAPI
    • Make sure to document as many aspects as possible, including all response status codes, parameters and attributes


API Design & Description in the Age of AI Thilo Frotscher & Erik Wilde: APIs für die AI Nutzung: Wie geht das?

(10) Creating APIs (in the old days…) #4

  • API documentation
    • Technical details missing in the API specification
    • Information explaining how endpoints work
    • Semantics of data structures and runtime behaviour
    • SDKs, quick-start guides


API Design & Description in the Age of AI Thilo Frotscher & Erik Wilde: APIs für die AI Nutzung: Wie geht das?

(11) Creating APIs (in the old days…) #5

  • Make APIs discoverable
    • Register APIs in dev portals, API marketplaces, or internal catalogs
    • Provide metadata, tags and standardized authentication details
    • Offer sandbox environments and test credentials
    • Maintain backwards compatibility


API Design & Description in the Age of AI Thilo Frotscher & Erik Wilde: APIs für die AI Nutzung: Wie geht das?

(12) Today: AI Everywhere

  • AI Coding Agents generate code that consumes APIs
  • AI Business Agents discover and consume APIs autonomously
  • What does all this mean for API providers?
    • What changes, what stays the same?


Exposing API Landscapes for AI Consumption

Outline (Exposing API Landscapes for AI Consumption)

  1. Introduction [3]
  2. API Design & Description in the Age of AI [6]
  3. Exposing API Landscapes for AI Consumption [9]
  4. MCP to the Rescue!?! [4]
  5. Conclusions [2]
Exposing API Landscapes for AI Consumption Thilo Frotscher & Erik Wilde: APIs für die AI Nutzung: Wie geht das?

(14) AI Integration Is the New Moat



Exposing API Landscapes for AI Consumption Thilo Frotscher & Erik Wilde: APIs für die AI Nutzung: Wie geht das?

(15) When LLMs Read Your APIs

  • APIs were typically consumed by developers
  • Developers write code that often fills DX gaps
  • LLMs have more limited context than developers
    • Coding agents shouldn't depend on developers selecting APIs
    • Business agents need to fully understand APIs


Exposing API Landscapes for AI Consumption Thilo Frotscher & Erik Wilde: APIs für die AI Nutzung: Wie geht das?

(16) Four Levels of API Evolution

  1. Improve descriptions → easy, no code change, immediately more agent-friendly
  2. Improve definitions (backwards-compatible) → change the API, don't break consumers
  3. Revise the API → new API; consumers must migrate
  4. Add workflows on top → orchestration above existing APIs


Exposing API Landscapes for AI Consumption Thilo Frotscher & Erik Wilde: APIs für die AI Nutzung: Wie geht das?

(17) Level 1a: Do Your DX Homework

  • Most API descriptions need quality improvements anyway
  • Better descriptions benefit everyone, not just AI agents
    • Complete and meaningful operation summaries
    • Comprehensive examples for all operations
    • Clear error handling
    • Consistent terminology and naming
  • Good DX is the foundation for good AX


Exposing API Landscapes for AI Consumption Thilo Frotscher & Erik Wilde: APIs für die AI Nutzung: Wie geht das?

(18) Level 1b: Now There's AX Homework, Too

  • AI agents need information developers don't typically require:
    • Everything that developers usually just know
    • Treat every API like an external one for developers with no context
  • OpenAPI Initiative (OAI) might add AX-specific fields
  • Until then, leverage description fields strategically


Exposing API Landscapes for AI Consumption Thilo Frotscher & Erik Wilde: APIs für die AI Nutzung: Wie geht das?

(19) Level 2: Backwards-Compatible Evolution

  • Improve the API itself without breaking existing consumers
    • Add optional fields and parameters
    • Enrich responses with new optional structures
    • Add new endpoints alongside existing ones
    • Tighten constraints; never loosen contracts
  • Works well in well-governed API landscapes
  • Morphs into Level 3 in brittle or ungoverned API landscapes


Exposing API Landscapes for AI Consumption Thilo Frotscher & Erik Wilde: APIs für die AI Nutzung: Wie geht das?

(20) Level 4: (Arazzo) Workflows

  • Declarative: define the data flow and success criteria
  • OpenAPI Initiative (OAI) [https://www.openapis.org/] standard for API workflows
  • References operationIds from OpenAPI/AsyncAPI documents 
workflows:
  - workflowId: weekend-getaway
    steps:
      - stepId: listHotels
        description: Retrieve hotels available in the destination city
        operationPath: '{$sourceDescriptions.liteapi-hotel-data.url}#/paths/~1data~1hotels/get'
        parameters:
          - name: cityName
            in: query
            value: $inputs.cityName
        successCriteria:
          - condition: $statusCode == 200
        outputs:
          hotelIds: $response.body#/data[*]/hotelId
  • arazzo.yaml

See the full workflow: arazzo-ui.jentic.com [https://arazzo-ui.jentic.com/?document=https%3A%2F%2Fraw.githubusercontent.com%2Ffrankkilcommins%2Fagentic-tools-webinar%2Frefs%2Fheads%2Fmain%2Farazzo%2Fweekend-getaway.arazzo.yaml]



Exposing API Landscapes for AI Consumption Thilo Frotscher & Erik Wilde: APIs für die AI Nutzung: Wie geht das?

(21) You Can't Improve What You Don't Measure

  • Spectral & Co. aren't all you need
  • API Scorecard scores your APIs across six dimensions
  • Web: jentic.com/scorecard [https://jentic.com/scorecard]
  • CLI: npm install -g @jentic/api-scorecard-cli
  • Repo: github.com/jentic/jentic-api-scorecard [https://github.com/jentic/jentic-api-scorecard]


Exposing API Landscapes for AI Consumption Thilo Frotscher & Erik Wilde: APIs für die AI Nutzung: Wie geht das?

(22) AI Readiness Score for APIs



MCP to the Rescue!?!

Outline (MCP to the Rescue!?!)

  1. Introduction [3]
  2. API Design & Description in the Age of AI [6]
  3. Exposing API Landscapes for AI Consumption [9]
  4. MCP to the Rescue!?! [4]
  5. Conclusions [2]
MCP to the Rescue!?! Thilo Frotscher & Erik Wilde: APIs für die AI Nutzung: Wie geht das?

(24) Just Use MCP?

  • The pitch: APIs are old, MCP is the future
  • Wrap anything in MCP → call it AI-ready
  • Skip the hard API work → straight to AI
  • Just start fresh after 10 years of API landscape work?


MCP to the Rescue!?! Thilo Frotscher & Erik Wilde: APIs für die AI Nutzung: Wie geht das?

(25) Problem 1: Directly Exposing APIs

  • Auto-generate MCP from OpenAPI for every API
  • Context window explosion on real workloads
  • Bad descriptions → bad MCP
  • AI still has to invent workflows across endpoints
  • Now your agents get to have a poor integration experience


MCP to the Rescue!?! Thilo Frotscher & Erik Wilde: APIs für die AI Nutzung: Wie geht das?

(26) Problem 2: Investing in the MCP Layer

  • Hand-craft MCP servers, design the best tools
  • The investment goes into the MCP layer
  • The capability layer underneath remains untouched
  • Better capabilities benefit everyone, not just MCP
  • Everybody's integration experience should benefit


MCP to the Rescue!?! Thilo Frotscher & Erik Wilde: APIs für die AI Nutzung: Wie geht das?

(27) MCP-Washing

  • Skip the API work → jump to MCP → call it AI-readiness
  • This is the wrong place to invest from a strategy perspective
  • MCP should be a thin layer, not a fat one
  • Invest in your APIs and then expose them through MCP


Conclusions

Outline (Conclusions)

  1. Introduction [3]
  2. API Design & Description in the Age of AI [6]
  3. Exposing API Landscapes for AI Consumption [9]
  4. MCP to the Rescue!?! [4]
  5. Conclusions [2]
Conclusions Thilo Frotscher & Erik Wilde: APIs für die AI Nutzung: Wie geht das?

(29) Aligned with the iSAQB API Module

  • Covered in the CPSA Advanced Level: API Module [https://www.isaqb.org/certifications/cpsa-certifications/cpsa-advanced-level/api/]
    • API Design, Description, Lifecycle
    • API Security & APIs at Scale
  • Background reading (iSAQB blog posts)
    • APIs as Business Assets: Designing for Scale, Governance, and AI [https://www.isaqb.org/blog/apis-as-business-assets-designing-for-scale-governance-and-ai/]
    • APIs as Digital Building Blocks [https://www.isaqb.org/blog/apis-digital-building-blocks-for-modern-business-models/]


Conclusions Thilo Frotscher & Erik Wilde: APIs für die AI Nutzung: Wie geht das?

(30) Thank You!
